title: Podman powershell completion script doesn't work for podman without '.exe'
body:
### Issue Description

Podman powershell completion script  is not setup to support `podman` as well and instead only supports `podman.exe`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Invoke `podman completion powershell   | Out-String | Invoke-Expression`
2. Type `podman.exe [tab]` > Works
3. Type `podman [tab]` > Doesn't work.


### Describe the results you received

- No autocomplete support for podman without '.exe'

### Describe the results you expected

- Autocomplete support without '.exe'

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

-

### Additional information

Possible solutions:

Append `Register-ArgumentCompleter -CommandName 'podman' -ScriptBlock ${__podman.exeCompleterBlock}` to the result of `podman completion powershell` in addition to `Register-ArgumentCompleter -CommandName 'podman' -ScriptBlock ${__podman.exeCompleterBlock}`

***or***

change `-CommandName 'podman.exe'` to include `podman` since it's a string array.
```
❯❯ Get-Help -Full Register-ArgumentCompleter

NAME
    Register-ArgumentCompleter

SYNTAX
    Register-ArgumentCompleter -CommandName <string[]> -ScriptBlock <scriptblock> [-Native] [<CommonParameters>]

    Register-ArgumentCompleter -ParameterName <string> -ScriptBlock <scriptblock> [-CommandName <string[]>] [<CommonParameters>]
```

### podman info output

comments.body: What is your podman client version? That has been fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/a91aa36367316ea24a07fde15689b7d738fc2cdb AFAICT

**Yup, 5.4 fixes the issue lol.**



Just ran the update on Podman Desktop today. Kinda weird that it doesn't seem to update podman client since the newest is indeed 5.4.

~Will try updating client and post an update.~
